extra.reasoning:
The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent). The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t34 (on send_attempt if opted_out and stage>=10). Since stage max is 10, t34 fires only when stage==10 and opted_out is true. This state is reachable.

Stage advances along a key-sequence chain (any wrong key resets to 0): stage 0->1 needs key_0 (t3), 1->2 key_1 (t6), 2->3 key_2 (t9), 3->4 key_1 (t12), 4->5 key_2 (t15), 5->6 key_1 (t18), 6->7 key_1 (t21), 7->8 key_2 (t24), 8->9 key_0 (t27), 9->10 key_1 (t30). Crucially, optout (t1) sets opted_out:=true but does not modify stage, so stage==10 is preserved after opting out.

Execution trace (12 events, within the 13-event bound), state shown as (opted_out, sent, stage):
Init (F,F,0)
1 key_0 -> t3 (F,F,1)
2 key_1 -> t6 (F,F,2)
3 key_2 -> t9 (F,F,3)
4 key_1 -> t12 (F,F,4)
5 key_2 -> t15 (F,F,5)
6 key_1 -> t18 (F,F,6)
7 key_1 -> t21 (F,F,7)
8 key_2 -> t24 (F,F,8)
9 key_0 -> t27 (F,F,9)
10 key_1 -> t30 (F,F,10)
11 optout -> t1 (T,F,10)
12 send_attempt -> t34 fires (guard opted_out and stage>=10 holds; t2 disabled since opted_out, t33 disabled since stage not <10): (T,T,10)

Final state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating opted_out implies not sent. At each event exactly one transition is enabled, so the trace is deterministic. Property is VIOLATED.
